item.title: A Hidden Russian Hand in Hungary’s Election? Actually, It’s Quite Open.
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has made hostility to Ukraine a centerpiece of his campaign. Moscow seems determined to repay the favor.

item.title: Why Trees Are Key to Russia’s Spring Offensive in Ukraine
item.description: In the age of drone warfare, Russia is expected to exploit the return of vegetation to help conceal its troops.

item.title: Epstein in Paris: How a Sex Offender Hustled for Access to France’s Elite
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein spent his last days of freedom in Paris, meeting with influential figures. It was a playbook he used everywhere he lived to stamp a veneer of respectability on a life of sordid criminality.

item.title: A food and drink guide to Ojai, California
item.description: In this valley oasis, Marina O’Loughlin finds the food superb, the bedtimes early and the locals determined to keep it that way

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel and Iran Trade Attacks After Trump’s New Hormuz Ultimatum
item.description: The fighting on Monday morning followed President Trump’s threat to bomb power plants and bridges on Tuesday if Iran did not open the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
US employment: more sludge
item.description: Plus, guns and butter redux

item.title: Trump vows to destroy Iran’s power plants and bridges unless it reopens strait
item.description: As war enters sixth week, the price of Brent crude rises slightly above $110 a barrel

item.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
item.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

item.title: FirstFT: EU warns of excessive energy support measures
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Private equity buyouts and Ukrainian drone campaign

item.title: Have markets already tightened the screw enough?
item.description: March’s hawkish communications in Europe have had the effect of tightening monetary policy

item.title: This Is Not China’s War, but Beijing Started Preparing for It Years Ago
item.description: Long concerned about geopolitical crises, China redoubled efforts to secure energy security when President Trump started raising the stakes in his first term.

item.title: Bank of England divided over how to tackle energy-induced inflation
item.description: After a rare unanimous vote to hold interest rates in March, conflicting approaches are likely at this month’s MPC meeting

item.title: Jones steps back from Downing St role as Romeo’s influence grows
item.description: Chief secretary to prime minister is less central to Starmer’s Number 10 operation after arrival of new head of civil service

item.title: Why this oil shock is different
item.description: Governments and central banks are out of policy ammunition to contain the economic fallout

item.title: Trump family-linked investment group backs spa company offering medical screening
item.description: XWell plans to roll out AI-powered infectious disease tool at airports during World Cup

item.title: EU warns capitals against turning energy crunch into fiscal crisis
item.description: Excessive spending to cushion soaring prices would have ‘serious fiscal implications’, says economy commissioner

item.title: Maga stands by Trump on Iran — for now
item.description: On the streets of small-town Georgia, the president’s base is backing the war as swing voters waver

item.title: Russian crypto payment system expands into Africa
item.description: Videos show A7 office in Nigeria, while company also claims new branch in Zimbabwe

item.title: Boring stocks are still beating the herd
item.description: Index of under-reported UK equities has done better than the FTSE 100

item.title: Ukraine’s drones dent Russia’s war-fuelled oil windfall
item.description: Disrupted exports add pressure to markets already reeling from Iran conflict

item.title: ‘We’re at a tipping point’: the defence boom set to reinvigorate Plymouth
item.description: Russian threat has guaranteed UK city a stream of military revenues stretching until 2070

item.title: Private equity buyouts slump as AI fears and war dent dealmaking
item.description: Groups agreed acquisitions worth $172bn in three months to March, a 36 per cent fall from previous quarter

item.title: Power-starved Cuba deepens reliance on Chinese solar tech
item.description: Imports of solar technology have soared, offering some respite for country that now faces near-total oil blockade

item.title: Bosses can help ease Britain’s sickness crisis, says former John Lewis chair
item.description: Government-backed plan will encourage employers to give staff better health support in the workplace

item.title: China and Europe launch rare joint space mission
item.description: Solar-focused project seeks to understand geomagnetic storms that can disrupt terrestrial communications

item.title: Let technology explore what the voters really want
item.description: New insights into motivation and strength of feeling could help democracy work better

item.title: Three Charged With Arson in Attack on U.K. Jewish Charity’s Ambulances
item.description: A fourth suspect was arrested after showing up at a hearing for the defendants, the police said. The March attack was widely condemned but has not been declared a terrorist incident.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: President Trump taunted Iran on social media, while strikes continued in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and some Gulf states.

item.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
item.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

item.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to cope with child's autism in a country with little help
item.description: Malawian Martha Ongwane, brought low by her daughter's autism, found a rare support group.

item.title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
item.description: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

item.title: Two protests, two elections: How Nepal's Gen Z succeeded where Bangladesh's stumbled
item.description: How did a youth movement triumph in one country but fall so short in another?

item.title: You can hire house help in 15 minutes in India - is it fair?
item.description: Instant house help brings speed and structure, but workers face tighter control and rising pressure in their jobs.

item.title: 'We are at the edge of a battlefield': BBC reports near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The BBC's Orla Guerin travels to the edge of the critical waterway that Iran has put a stranglehold on.

item.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
item.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

item.title: What We Know About the Rescue of a U.S. Airman After Two Jet Crashes
item.description: An airman was rescued on Saturday night, President Trump said. The rescue came after a risky two-day operation deep inside Iran.

item.title: Pepsi withdraws as UK festival sponsor after Kanye West backlash
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer says it is "deeply concerning" the rapper is set to headline a festival after recent antisemitic comments.

item.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
item.description: The incident comes a week before the polls, and follow warnings of a potential operations staged to influence voters.

item.title: Pepsi Drops Sponsorship of Wireless Festival Headlined by Kanye West
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was “deeply concerned” that the rapper known for antisemitic and racist comments had been booked to perform at the Wireless Festival.

item.title: Iran’s Downing of Fighter Jet and U.S. Rescue Leave Both Sides Dangerously Emboldened
item.description: After Iran shot down a U.S. plane and U.S. forces pulled off a risky ground operation to extricate a stranded airman, both sides claimed victory. That confidence could fuel further escalation.

item.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
item.description: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

item.title: More Than 70 Migrants Missing After Boat Capsizes Off Libya
item.description: Survivors said at least 100 people were on a boat that had left Libya for Europe. Only 32 of those aboard are known to have survived.

item.title: The South American petro-state profiting from the Iran war
item.description: The boom is raising the risk that oil money devours the economy

item.title: Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass
item.description: Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff.

item.title: European allies are losing hope of keeping America in NATO
item.description: Donald Trump is furious at their refusal to help his war against Iran

item.title: Interceptor Missiles Save Lives, but Stockpiles Are Dwindling
item.description: Defense systems that shoot projectiles out of the sky have become one of the most important components of warfare, so much so that militaries face a diminishing supply.

item.title: The tumbling rupee is a big problem for Narendra Modi
item.description: India’s prime minister knows the risks of a weak currency

item.title: China Built the World’s Drone Industry. Now It’s Locking Down the Skies.
item.description: The Chinese government tightened rules to curb what it described as illegal drone use, but some users say the changes are now restricting too many flights.

item.title: Dozens Killed in Haiti Massacre as International Force Trickles In
item.description: Gangs tore through several rural communities last weekend, underscoring the challenges that will face the new, U.N.-backed Gang Suppression Force starting to enter the country.

item.title: Comedians tell ministers lack of funding is no laughing matter
item.description: Culture Minister Ian Murray has agreed to work with the comedy industry to boost support after talks.

item.title: The man who became one of India's greatest stage queens
item.description: Chapal Bhaduri was Bengal’s biggest stage queen - until women arrived and the spotlight moved on.

item.title: Trump Wants to Make Deportation Deals. Autocrats Are Ready to Listen.
item.description: The White House has turned deportations, a signature domestic issue, into a major piece of foreign policy. Here’s what we know about the program.

item.title: Deadly Earthquake and Floods Worsen Afghanistan’s Troubles
item.description: Floods have killed at least 77 people this week and destroyed or damaged thousands of homes, and an earthquake on Friday killed a dozen more.

item.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
item.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

item.title: German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad
item.description: Under the law, travel approvals must generally be granted and it remains unclear how the rule would be enforced if breached.

item.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
item.description: A Russian drone hit a busy spot in the southern Ukrainian town on Saturday morning, injuring another 21 people.

item.title: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
item.description: Cameroon's President Paul Biya, 93, won his eighth successive term last year in a disputed election.

item.title: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
item.description: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

item.title: Europe’s Options in the Strait of Hormuz: Few, and Risky
item.description: European leaders and other officials have ideas for bringing shipping back to the strait once the Iran war ends. But none of them are sure bets.

item.title: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
item.description: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

item.title: Football rally in Peru leaves one dead and dozens injured
item.description: Hundreds of Alianza Lima fans had gathered for a pregame rally ahead of a match against local rivals Universitario de Deportes.

item.title: 'Not fit for purpose' - the secret history of a deadly phrase
item.description: The four word phrase - first uttered by Labour Home Secretary John Reid - has become a by-word for incompetence.

item.title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
item.description: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.

item.title: The Art of No Deal: Trump’s Approach to the Iran War
item.description: “The plan is not to have a plan,” the staff writer Susan B. Glasser says.

item.title: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
item.description: People evicted to build Nigeria's capital accuse the government of failing to fulfill its promises to them.

item.title: How Bombay went from a fort city to a bustling metropolis
item.description: A new exhibition charts Bombay's passage through the centuries via paintings, photographs and prints.

item.title: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
item.description: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

item.title: After 16 years in power, could Viktor Orban finally be unseated?
item.description: Hungary is going to the polls in nine days - after 16 years in power, can Viktor Orban be unseated?

item.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
item.description: US President Trump has made clear his desire to change Cuba's leadership and has placed the country under an oil embargo.

item.title: A captive American in Iran could lead to further escalation
item.description: It could prompt Donald Trump to follow through on his worst threats

item.title: US warns its citizens to leave Lebanon citing escalating security risks
item.description: The advisory follows recent military developments, including a US jet reportedly being shot down and Israeli strikes on parts of Beirut.

item.title: Unanswered questions remain after Australia's most wanted fugitive killed in standoff
item.description: Double-murderer Dezi Freeman evaded capture for seven months in the bush but police believe he had help.

item.title: Marmalades may need to be relabelled under post-Brexit food deal
item.description: The breakfast favourite will be legally renamed when Britain aligns with new EU labelling rules.

item.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

item.title: US deports eight people 'of African origin' to Uganda
item.description: The Uganda Law Society criticises the deportation, saying it was illegal and "dehumanising".

item.title: Italy's Uffizi Galleries targeted in cyber-attack but deny security breach
item.description: Hackers were reported to have infiltrated IT systems - but the gallery says its works are safe.

item.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
item.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

item.title: France's Muslim gathering ban overturned by courts
item.description: The Paris police department had argued that the four-day gathering was a security threat because it could be a target of terrorism.

item.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
item.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

item.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
item.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

item.title: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
item.description: Capt Ibrahim Traoré, who seized power in 2023, says democratic rule would not work for his country.

item.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
item.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

item.title: The 'Swiss Leicester' closing on their first title
item.description: A decade after Leicester unexpectedly won the Premier League, FC Thun of Switzerland are closing on one of football's greatest underdog triumphs.
